title: cirrhopetalum viscidum
intro: Cirrhopetalum viscidum, also known as the hairy orchid or sticky orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cirrhopetalum viscidum include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to lower fever and reduce pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
